revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23991619004|time triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:飯島八幡神社]]
{{otheruses|the Iijima Hachiman Shrine located in Iijima, Shirakawa Village|the Hachiman Shrine in Ogimachi, Shirakawa Village|Shirakawa Hachiman Shrine|the Hachiman Shrine in Hatogaya, Shirakawa Village|Hatogaya Hachiman Shrine}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Iijima Hachiman Shrine | image = [[File:Iijima hachiman shrine.jpg|center|250px|]] | location = Iijima, Shirakawa Village, Ōno District, Gifu Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|36|16|46|N|136|54|2|E|scale:25000|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|qid=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin|1=Emperor Ōjin}} | Status = {{ill|Ginpeisha|en|銀幣社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ginpeisha|qq=}} | founded = {{nihongo|Kan'ei 3|寛永3rd year }} (1626) }}
'''Iijima Hachiman Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|飯島八幡神社}}, ''Iijima Hachiman Jinja'') is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Shirakawa, Gifu|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirakawa, Gifu|qq=}}, {{ill|Ōno District, Gifu|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno District, Gifu|qq=}}. It is situated within the {{ill|World Heritage Site|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/World Heritage Site|qq=}} of {{ill|Shirakawa-gō|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirakawa-gō|qq=}}, directly opposite the {{ill|Roadside Station Shirakawa-gō|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Roadside Station Shirakawa-gō|qq=}}.
Historically, Shirakawa-gō encompassed the entire {{ill|Shō River|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shō River|qq=}} basin in {{ill|Hida Province|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hida Province|qq=}}. In {{nihongo|1875|明治8th year }}, administrative changes divided the region: the 23 villages of "Lower Shirakawa-gō," north of the now-submerged Ojin Village (due to the {{ill|Miboro Dam|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miboro Dam|qq=}}), became Shirakawa Village, while the 18 villages of "Upper Shirakawa-gō" to the south, near Kaijō Village (also submerged by the dam), became {{ill|Shōkawa Village|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōkawa Village|qq=}}, now part of {{ill|Takayama, Gifu|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takayama, Gifu|qq=}}.
The shrine is known for hosting its annual {{ill|Doburoku festival|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Doburoku festival|qq=}} in mid-October. This festival is held third in sequence after those at {{ill|Shirakawa Hachiman Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirakawa Hachiman Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Hatogaya Hachiman Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatogaya Hachiman Shrine|qq=}}.
=== deity === * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|qid=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin|1=Emperor Ōjin}}
=== History === {{empty section|date=2020-10}}
=== Cultural Properties === ; Shirakawa Village Designated Cultural Properties * **Doburoku festival** (Intangible Folk Cultural Property)<ref name="shirakawamura bunkazai">{{Cite web|url=http://shirakawa-go.org/kankou/siru/bunka/666/|title=Cultural Properties of Shirakawa Village|publisher=Shirakawa Village|accessdate=2013-04-20}}</ref> * **Enoki Tree at Iijima Hachiman Shrine** (Natural Monument)<ref name="shirakawamura bunkazai"/>
=== location === ; location * Iijima, Shirakawa Village, Ōno District, Gifu Prefecture
=== Transportation === ==== By Car ==== * Approximately 50 meters from {{ill|Shirakawa-gō Interchange|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirakawa-gō Interchange|qq=}} on the {{ill|Tōkai-Hokuriku Expressway|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkai-Hokuriku Expressway|qq=}}.
==== By Public Transportation ==== * Bus: Take the {{ill|Nohi Bus|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nohi Bus|qq=}} Shirakawa-gō/Kanazawa Line from {{ill|Takaoka Station|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaoka Station|qq=}} ({{ill|Ainokaze Toyama Railway|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ainokaze Toyama Railway|qq=}}) or {{ill|Takayama Nohi Bus Center|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takayama Nohi Bus Center|qq=}}, alighting at Iijima.
=== Additional Information === * The shrine is said to have inspired the ''ritual implements hall'' depicted in the {{ill|dōjin game|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/dōjin game|qq=}} series {{ill|Higurashi no Naku Koro ni|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higurashi no Naku Koro ni|qq=}}.
== References == {{reflist}}
